prompt.content:
I'm analyzing payment patterns for our collections team to identify subscribers with consistent payment behavior who might qualify for credit limit increases. We're using invoice data from 2024 and 2025.

For subscribers on postpaid plans who have had at least 5 invoices during this period (based on invoice due dates falling in 2024-2025), calculate the following metrics across all their invoices:

-Total amount invoiced to these subscribers
-Total amount actually paid by these subscribers
-Overall payment rate as a percentage (total paid / total invoiced)
-Average payment coverage ratio per qualifying subscriber (calculated as total paid divided by total invoiced for each subscriber, then averaged)
-Count of distinct subscribers meeting the 5-invoice minimum

Focus on understanding payment completeness to help us assess creditworthiness. The coverage ratio metric helps us identify if subscribers are consistently paying their full balances. Use the tables subscriber_plans, subscription_plans, invoices, and payment_transactions (join invoices to payments on invoice_id). Round monetary values to 2 decimal places, percentages and ratios to 1 decimal place. Provide your full SQLite query and the final results.

rubrics.name: Reports total amount invoiced as 122578 (strict threshold) (ver_f5049f3842e841f89c29c72962f648ca)

messages.content: Reports total amount invoiced as 122578 (strict threshold).

rubrics.name: Reports average payment coverage ratio as 0.8 (+/- 0.05) (ver_b483e7a977f4487ebef52b996782d9b5)

messages.content: Reports average payment coverage ratio as 0.8 (+/- 0.05).

rubrics.name: Reports count of distinct qualifying subscribers as 312 (strict threshold)... (ver_8f622eadd0434d13b97331933680fb69)

messages.content: Reports count of distinct qualifying subscribers as 312 (strict threshold) (acceptable range 309 to 315).

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
total_invoiced
122578

total_paid
102704.05

payment_rate_pct
83.8

avg_coverage_ratio
0.8

qualifying_subscriber_count
312

``` sql 

WITH PostpaidSubscribers AS (
    -- Identify subscribers on postpaid plans with at least 5 invoices in 2024-2025
    SELECT i.subscriber_id
    FROM invoices i
    JOIN subscriber_plans sp ON i.subscriber_id = sp.subscriber_id
    JOIN subscription_plans p ON sp.plan_id = p.plan_id
    WHERE p.plan_type = 'postpaid'
      AND i.due_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2025-12-31'
    GROUP BY i.subscriber_id
    HAVING COUNT(i.invoice_id) >= 5
),
InvoiceLevelPayments AS (
    -- Aggregate payments at invoice level to handle multiple payments per invoice
    SELECT
        i.invoice_id,
        i.subscriber_id,
        i.total_amount AS invoice_amount,
        SUM(CASE WHEN pt.transaction_status = 'success' THEN pt.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_paid
    FROM invoices i
    JOIN PostpaidSubscribers ps ON i.subscriber_id = ps.subscriber_id
    LEFT JOIN payment_transactions pt ON i.invoice_id = pt.invoice_id
    WHERE i.due_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2025-12-31'
    GROUP BY i.invoice_id, i.subscriber_id, i.total_amount
),
SubscriberPaymentRatios AS (
    -- Calculate per-subscriber coverage ratio
    SELECT
        subscriber_id,
        SUM(total_paid) / SUM(invoice_amount) AS coverage_ratio
    FROM InvoiceLevelPayments
    GROUP BY subscriber_id
)
SELECT
    ROUND(SUM(ilp.invoice_amount), 2) AS total_invoiced,
    ROUND(SUM(ilp.total_paid), 2) AS total_paid,
    ROUND(SUM(ilp.total_paid) * 100.0 / SUM(ilp.invoice_amount), 1) AS payment_rate_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(spr.coverage_ratio), 1) AS avg_coverage_ratio,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ilp.subscriber_id) AS qualifying_subscriber_count
FROM InvoiceLevelPayments ilp
JOIN SubscriberPaymentRatios spr ON ilp.subscriber_id = spr.subscriber_id;

```
